items.title: Humble containers in WA's outskirts herald a new global energy era

children.content: Western Australia in particular is proving to the world that grid-scale batteries are no longer a complementary technology but can play a central role in power grids.

gallery.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset

thumbnail.altText: rows of battery containers at sunset
A man in high-vis wearing a hard hat stands before rows of white shipping-container-sized batteries

items.title: Refinery on WA's south coast says it could replace region's diesel with biofuel

children.content: In the 1990s, Esperance was seen as a key to a major biofuels industry for Western Australia. Thirty years later, the industry has seen little change. But some say the area has untapped potential as a major fuel supplier.

items.title: Social media group calls for change while exposing inequality in sport

children.content: Not All Clubs calls out inequality in sporting organisations across the country. This weekend, the group is calling on teams to wear red armbands in memory of the women whose lives have been claimed by gender-based violence this year.

image.altText: Two AFL players stand in a huddle. Both players wear two red bands on their left arm.

items.title: Specific needs 'soar' across school system over past decade, principal says

children.content: WA teachers will be taught how to better support children with higher needs amid rising rates of disability diagnoses, after a report found public schools needed to do more.

gallery.altText: Students are having a math class.
Two girls are having a class in a classroom.
A principal smiles to his students in a classroom.

thumbnail.altText: Students are having a math class.
Two girls are having a class in a classroom.
A principal smiles to his students in a classroom.

items.title: Council buys land for $1, but what's the catch?

children.content: A Midwest city council buys Crown land for $1 to develop more than 100 dwellings to house key workers, but WA's shadow local government minister says it is an example of more responsibility being placed on councils.

image.altText: A drone image of a block of land with bushland on surrounded by buildings, road and the beach.

items.title: Police divers join search for missing swimmer in WA's South West

children.content: Police say the woman entered the water at Koombana Bay just after 9:20am and has not been seen since

image.altText: Police divers arrive at the scene in Koombana Bay in Bunbury.

items.title: Rodent faeces, animal blood, mould spark $60k penalty for country bakery

children.content: Inspectors found animal blood dripping onto bread and rodent faeces on food at the Albany French Hot Bread Shop, triggering prosecution and $60,000 in penalties for the owners.

gallery.altText: A wide of a shop front

thumbnail.altText: A wide of a shop front

items.title: Student says WA school closed by toxic baits had been overrun by mice

children.content: A year 12 student at the WA College of Agriculture Morawa says there were fears of "cooking" mice in the heating system before the mistaken deployment of toxic baits shut the school down.

gallery.altText: A sign for The Western Australian College of Agriculture Morawa campus in wood and gold metal
A close up of dead mice laying on the brown dirt
A mouse on a stove; a mouse up a wall

thumbnail.altText: A sign for The Western Australian College of Agriculture Morawa campus in wood and gold metal
A close up of dead mice laying on the brown dirt
A mouse on a stove; a mouse up a wall

items.title: ABC bosses tell Senate estimates it is time to enter 'new phase' after news director resigns

children.content: Managing director Hugh Marks tells senators the ABC must adapt for the future. Follow live.

image.altText: Marks sits at a desk awaiting questioning

items.title: BP removes chair over 'serious concerns' about conduct

children.content: The BBC reported Albert Manifold's departure followed concerns about "bullying" and "overbearing" behaviour, which he later described as a "false narrative".

image.altText: A man smiling in a suit.

items.title: UK's social media ban could be harsher than Australia's. Here's how it compares

children.content: The UK could become the latest nation to implement a social media ban, after British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer vowed to introduce a "game changer" policy.

image.altText: Keir Starmer in a dark shirt with his sleeves rolled up, siting while smiling next to two children with red hats.

items.title: Why this photo has been terrifying the internet for years

children.content: Forget about slashers and body horror — the next evolution of fright stems from an fluorescent-lit room.

items.title: The BHP files reveal a big problem facing the mining giant

children.content: For a company that prides itself on digging out the metals for the world's clean energy future, saying nothing about its own clean energy future is not an option.

articles.title: WA iron ore processing project dumped by BHP for not being profitable enough

children.content: Internal BHP documents reveal how the mining giant planned to set up an iron ore processing facility in the Pilbara to cut carbon emissions before backing away from it entirely.

image.altText: A photo of an iron ore mine is ripped down the middle and replaced by a photo of a BHP building.

articles.title: Leaked documents about WA mines reveal how far BHP really is from net zero goals

children.content: The mining giant's delay in the rollout of renewable energy and a fleet of electric trucks and trains in the Pilbara has raised doubts inside the company about it meeting its ambitious climate goal.

image.altText: A large yellow mining truck drives along a raise road. It has a load in its tray.

gallery.altText: Mining infrastructure and machinery in pits of red dirt.
Three newspapers with the same ad which says 'BHP has reduced emissions by 36 per cent' and has graphs.

thumbnail.altText: Mining infrastructure and machinery in pits of red dirt.
Three newspapers with the same ad which says 'BHP has reduced emissions by 36 per cent' and has graphs.

items.title: Cuts of meat that will give you more bang for your buck

children.content: If you are looking to trim your household food budget, lower-cost cuts of meat could be a good place to start. Here are some tips on what to look for and how to cook them.

image.altText: Two women looking at a packet of pre-packaged meat at a supermarket

items.title: ASX set to open lower as price of oil drops

children.content: The Aussie share market is set to open lower as the price of oil drops. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: As the debate heats up, migrants are quietly keeping the bush running

children.content: Amid ongoing discussion about Australia's immigration intake and migration policies, regional residents say many industries in the bush cannot survive without overseas workers.

image.altText: workers from the pacific in red dirt field planting trees with tractor in front of them

items.title: UK spy chief says 500,000 Russians killed in Ukraine war so far

children.content: The head of a major UK intelligence agency is warning that Moscow and other adversaries could use artificial intelligence and other emerging technology to conduct "hybrid warfare" against Western allies.

image.altText: A man stands in front of the Russian flag.

items.title: 'Inhumane' disability home had fridges locked as residents slept on floor

children.content: Former workers at a disability home in Western Australia's far north raise allegations of neglect, including medical mismanagement, locked fridges and a child being placed at risk.

gallery.altText: a house and a leafy front yard
a photo of a lock on fridge

thumbnail.altText: a house and a leafy front yard
a photo of a lock on fridge

items.title: Brad Scott confirms Essendon weighed up bumper Harley Reid offer

children.content: Former Essendon coach Brad Scott reveals Essendon weighed up tabling a 10-year, $22 million contract offer to West Coast star Harley Reid, and says he wanted the club to trade Zach Merrett last summer.

image.altText: Brad Scott pictured during his Essendon coaching tenure

items.title: FIFA faces investigation over 'impossibly high' World Cup ticket prices

children.content: In a joint statement, New Jersey's attorney-general accused the football governing body of having turned "buying a ticket to the World Cup into a gauntlet of confusion, fake scarcity, and impossibly high prices".

image.altText: The FIFA World Cup 2026 logo on the side of a stadium roof.

items.title: Kalshi and Polymarket are billion dollar bets banned from Australia — for now

children.content: Kalshi and Polymarket have gone from obscure start-ups to two of the world's biggest prediction markets where users bet on yes-or-no questions spanning politics, pop culture and war.

image.altText: A man speaking on stage at a lecturn.

items.title: Defence industry could rival WA resources sector under weapons hub plan

children.content: Missiles for use in the Australian military could soon be made in Western Australia with the government seeking to establish a local weapons manufacturing hub.

image.altText: A mid-shot of WA Premier Roger Cook giving a speech at an orange dais on stage wearing a suit and tie, against a black backdrop.

items.title: Farmers hopeful cold front will help WA's dry May problem

children.content: A downward trend in autumn rainfall has WA farmers adapting to early seeding, but hoping this week's forecast cold front will provide a much-needed break to the dry spell.

gallery.altText: A tractor pulling crop planting equipment in a paddock sits underneath a vibrant and stunning sunset
A photo of farmer Scott Crosby
A photo of Scott Crosby working on his farm at Nyabing

thumbnail.altText: A tractor pulling crop planting equipment in a paddock sits underneath a vibrant and stunning sunset
